name: Jean Carroll
blurb: After early success in vaudeville, she went solo in the 1940s and appeared on The Ed Sullivan Show more than 20 times, delivering polished, conversational routines about her husband, daughter, and shopping.
bio: Jean Carroll was an American comedian widely regarded as a pioneer of female stand-up comedy and among the first women to perform solo comic monologues on national television. After early success in vaudeville, she went solo in the 1940s and appeared on The Ed Sullivan Show more than 20 times, delivering polished, conversational routines about her husband, daughter, and shopping. She influenced later comedians including Joan Rivers and Lily Tomlin.

timeline.ev: Debuts on Toast of the Town, the original Ed Sullivan Show
Stars in her own sitcom The Jean Carroll Show
Performs her famous department-store dress routine on Ed Sullivan
Dies at age 98 in White Plains, New York

accolades: Cited as a pioneer of female stand-up comedy
One of the most frequent comedy guests on The Ed Sullivan Show
